options: A) The motion of planets
B) The atomic arrangement in crystalline solids
C) The chemistry of gases only
D) The genetics of living cells
questions.explanation: Crystallography focuses on how atoms, ions, or molecules are arranged in crystalline solids.

questions.questionText: Why are X-rays commonly used in crystallography?
options: A) Because X-rays have wavelengths comparable to atomic spacing
B) Because X-rays make crystals grow faster
C) Because X-rays change solids into liquids
D) Because X-rays only interact with metals
questions.explanation: X-rays are ideal because their wavelengths are similar to distances between atoms in crystals, allowing diffraction patterns to reveal structure.

questions.questionText: What happens when X-rays strike a crystal in the correct way?
options: A) The crystal disappears
B) The X-rays are diffracted into a pattern
C) The crystal becomes radioactive
D) The atoms stop vibrating
questions.explanation: The repeating structure of a crystal causes X-rays to diffract, creating a pattern that can be analyzed to determine atomic arrangement.

questions.questionText: In crystallography, what is a unit cell?
options: A) The largest visible crystal sample
B) The smallest repeating structural unit of a crystal
C) A damaged region inside a crystal
D) A tool used to cut crystals
questions.explanation: A unit cell is the smallest repeating three-dimensional pattern that builds the entire crystal lattice.

questions.questionText: Which property best describes a crystal lattice?
options: A) Random atomic arrangement
B) Repeating and ordered arrangement of particles
C) A liquid-like structure
D) A structure with no symmetry
questions.explanation: A crystal lattice is a highly ordered, repeating arrangement of particles in space.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is an important application of crystallography?
options: A) Predicting daily weather
B) Determining protein structures for medicine
C) Measuring sound frequency in music
D) Calculating internet speed
questions.explanation: Crystallography is widely used to determine the structures of proteins and other biomolecules, which is essential in drug design and medical research.

questions.questionText: What information can scientists most directly obtain from a diffraction pattern?
options: A) The exact color of a crystal
B) The arrangement of atoms inside the crystal
C) The age of the crystal in years
D) The crystal's temperature history only
questions.explanation: Diffraction patterns contain information about atomic spacing and symmetry, which allows scientists to infer internal structure.

questions.questionText: Why is symmetry important in crystallography?
options: A) It helps describe and classify crystal structures
B) It prevents crystals from breaking
C) It increases crystal temperature
D) It makes all crystals transparent
questions.explanation: Symmetry is a key feature used to classify crystals and understand how their atomic structures repeat in space.

questions.questionText: If a solid has no long-range repeating atomic order, it is best described as:
options: A) Crystalline
B) Metallic
C) Amorphous
D) Ionic
questions.explanation: Amorphous solids, such as glass, do not have the long-range ordered structure found in crystals.

questions.questionText: What is one major challenge in determining crystal structures experimentally?
options: A) Crystals always melt under light
B) High-quality crystals are often difficult to grow
C) X-rays cannot pass through solids
D) All crystals give the same diffraction pattern
questions.explanation: Accurate crystallographic analysis usually requires high-quality crystals, and growing suitable crystals can be difficult for many substances.

rewards.description: Earn 500 points for mastering crystal structure concepts.
